description: Make both CLI and sidebar plugin search robust and on-par: support full package names (scoped and unscoped), partial names (opentabs-plugin-*), and short names as search queries, ensuring identical search behavior in both paths.

userStories.title: Fix direct probe to try scoped variant when query starts with opentabs-plugin-
userStories.description: Both the server's probeDirectPackage and the CLI's buildDirectLookupCandidates only probe the query as-is when it starts with 'opentabs-plugin-'. Since all official plugins are scoped (@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-*), probing only the unscoped name fails. Both should also try the scoped variant.
acceptanceCriteria: Server: searching 'opentabs-plugin-notion' in the sidebar probes both @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-notion and opentabs-plugin-notion
CLI: searching 'opentabs-plugin-notion' probes both @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-notion and opentabs-plugin-notion
Server: searching '@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-notion' still probes only that exact name
CLI: searching '@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-notion' still probes only that exact name
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/plugin-management.ts (fix probeDirectPackage)
- platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts (fix buildDirectLookupCandidates)

Context:
Both functions have the same logic for when the query starts with `opentabs-plugin-`:

Server probeDirectPackage (line 209-213):
```
const candidates =
  query.startsWith('@') || query.startsWith('opentabs-plugin-')
    ? [query]
    : [`@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-${query}`, `opentabs-plugin-${query}`];
```

CLI buildDirectLookupCandidates (line 271-274):
```
if (query.startsWith('@') || query.startsWith(PLUGIN_PREFIX)) return [query];
return [`@opentabs-dev/${PLUGIN_PREFIX}${query}`, `${PLUGIN_PREFIX}${query}`];
```

Where PLUGIN_PREFIX = 'opentabs-plugin-'

Changes:

1. In server probeDirectPackage, change the candidates logic:
```
const candidates =
  query.startsWith('@')
    ? [query]
    : query.startsWith('opentabs-plugin-')
      ? [`@opentabs-dev/${query}`, query]
      : [`@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-${query}`, `opentabs-plugin-${query}`];
```

2. In CLI buildDirectLookupCandidates, change the logic:
```
if (query.startsWith('@')) return [query];
if (query.startsWith(PLUGIN_PREFIX)) return [`@opentabs-dev/${query}`, query];
return [`@opentabs-dev/${PLUGIN_PREFIX}${query}`, `${PLUGIN_PREFIX}${query}`];
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the `@`-prefixed case — a fully scoped name should probe exactly as-is
- The scoped variant must come first in the candidates list (try official packages before community packages)

userStories.title: Fix post-filter to also match against full package name
userStories.description: The post-filter in both server and CLI only checks extractShortName(name) and description against the query. When the query is a full or partial package name like 'opentabs-plugin-notion' or '@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-notion', extractShortName produces 'notion' which does not include the longer query string, so the filter rejects the match. The filter should also check the full package name.
acceptanceCriteria: Server: keyword search post-filter matches when the query appears in the full package name
CLI: keyword search post-filter matches when the query appears in the full package name
Searching 'opentabs-plugin-notion' returns the Notion plugin from keyword results (not just direct probe)
Searching 'notion' still works as before (short name match)
